mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of stable unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, boosting corporate profit forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports near-term equity price appreciation over a one-week horizon during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 5-day returns in >55% of observations, or the signal reverses direction during contractionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth reflects robust labor demand and rising household income, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative 5-day returns in >50% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal increased residential investment and construction activity, which amplifies economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the relationship breaks down during late-cycle peaks.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between slowing industrial production and rising unemployment indicates a supply-demand mismatch and economic deceleration, which triggers risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.
